name: Google Maps Search Export

request.description:
Use this format if you already have a direct Google Maps search or place URL.

#### 🧾 Notes:

* Only the `url` key is required.
* URLs must match Google Maps search or place format.
* Best for manual inputs or small-scale targeted searches.

Use this endpoint to fetch valid **regions**, **districts**, and **cities** used in parameter-based tasks for the **Google Maps Search Export** crawler.

This is essential when constructing structured tasks (`is_params: true`) where accurate location inputs are required.

### 🧾 **Query Parameters**

| Parameter | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **country_code** | ✅ yes | ISO 2-letter country code (e.g., `US`, `FR`, `PK`). |
| **region** | ⛔ conditional | Required when querying **districts** or **cities**. Leave empty to get **regions**. |
| **district** | ❌ optional | Further narrows the city list within a region. |
| **city** | ❌ optional | Used when retrieving a refined list of locations by district or postal grouping. |

> The value you leave empty determines what the API returns: 
  

### 📎 Use In Context

- Use the returned values for `region`, `district`, and `city` in parameter-based tasks (`is_params: true`).
    
- Combine them with other task fields like `category` to generate accurate and valid search inputs.
    
- Refer to:
    
    - [Add task params](#f05ba17b-0663-4cab-b714-88de9c090838) for how these values fit into your tasks.
        
    - [Get crawler parameters](#f0170945-174a-433b-b24d-b35731e78f4e) for all accepted fields and formats.

request.description:
Use this format to dynamically define search inputs using structured metadata like city, category, and country which can be obtained from [Geolocation Section](#2446c796-2263-4d38-b71e-ee9eb2729d42).

#### 🧾 Notes:

- **`city`** and **`category`** are **required**.
    
- **`country`**, `region`, and `district` are **optional**.
    
- Ideal for automation and scaling across regions.

response.name: Example

request.description: These `params` control how the **Google Maps Search Export** crawler behaves — including language, location context, filters, and optional data collection features. ➡️ These parameters are specific to the **Google Maps Search Export** crawler. _Refer to the_ [Constructing the params Object](#) _section to understand how to format and apply them._ | Parameter | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | **country** | string | Defines the regional context for search results. Example: `"United States"` returns results from a U.S.-based perspective. | | **ratings** | string | Filters places by rating threshold. Example: `"Any rating"`, `"4.0+"`, `"2.5+"`. | | **language** | string | Sets the language Google Maps uses to display place names, descriptions, and metadata. Example: `"English (United States)"`. | | **max_results** | integer | Maximum number of places to collect before the task ends. Example: `200`. | | **functions.collect_contacts** | boolean | If `true`: visits the business’s website (if available) to extract `email`, `facebook`, `instagram`, and `additional_phone`. | | **functions.images** | boolean | If `true`: collects all image URLs from the Google Place page. | | **functions.details** | boolean | If `true`: visits the Google Maps place page and collects additional metadata like `plus_code`, `poi`, `health`, and `last_opening_hours_updated_at`. | --- ### ⚠️ **Additional Notes** - Enabling `functions.collect_contacts`, `functions.images`, or `functions.details` **may incur additional credits**. - The field `export_unique_results: true` avoids duplicate entries when multiple places appear more than once in search results.

response.name: Example

request.description:
Following are the result fields that are crawled by the **Google Maps Search Export**.

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **id** | integer | Native identifier for the place (same as `native_id`). |
| **object** | string | Type of returned object (usually `"result"`). |
| **squid** | string | Secondary unique identifier for internal tracking. |
| **run** | string | Identifier for the scraping run this result belongs to. |
| **about** | string / null | General about/description field (if available). |
| **additional_phone** | string / null | Additional contact phone number. |
| **address** | string | Full physical address of the place. |
| **booking_link** | string | External booking URL, such as TheFork or other platforms. |
| **category** | string | Business category (e.g., Restaurant, Hotel, etc.). |
| **cid** | string | [Google Maps Customer ID](https://www.vendasta.com/blog/cid-number-google/). |
| **city** | string | City where the place is located. |
| **country_code** | string | ISO 2-letter country code. |
| **country_name** | string | Full country name. |
| **description** | string / null | Description of the place (if provided). |
| **email** | string / null | Business contact email (if available). |
| **facebook** | string / null | Facebook page link. |
| **functions** | json object | Metadata about the scraping run or feature flags (e.g., `filling_date`). |
| **has_owner** | boolean | Indicates if the place has a verified owner on Google. |
| **health** | string / null | Health/sanitation inspection info (rarely present). |
| **images** | string / null | Image list or gallery (structure may vary). |
| **images_count** | integer | Number of images available. |
| **instagram** | string / null | Instagram profile link. |
| **is_permanently_closed** | boolean | Whether the place is permanently closed. |
| **is_temporarily_closed** | boolean | Whether the place is temporarily closed. |
| **last_opening_hours_updated_at** | string / null | Last time opening hours were updated. |
| **lat** | float | Latitude of the place. |
| **lng** | float | Longitude of the place. |
| **main_image_url** | string | URL of the main cover image for the place. |
| **match_filters** | boolean | Whether this result matches any filter applied during scraping. |
| **menu** | string / null | Link to the place’s menu (if any). |
| **name** | string | Name of the place. |
| **native_id** | integer | Native identifier (same as `id`). |
| **no_match_reasons** | string | Explanation if the result didn't match filters. |
| **opening_hours** | string | Weekly opening hours in text format. |
| **phone** | string | Primary business phone number. |
| **plus_code** | string / null | Google Maps Plus Code (if available). |
| **poi** | string / null | Point of Interest reference (rarely populated). |
| **popular_times** | string | Popular visiting times (raw data string, may need parsing). |
| **price** | string / null | Price level or range, if available. |
| **ratings** | integer | Total number of user reviews/ratings. |
| **score** | float | Average user rating (e.g., 4.8). |
| **scraping_time** | timestamp | When this data was scraped. |
| **url** | string | Full Google Maps URL to the place. |
| **website** | string / null | Official website of the business. |
| **zero_x** | string | Google Maps place unique hash. |
| **zip_code** | string | ZIP/postal code. |

description:
The **Google Maps Search Export** crawler supports **two types of task inputs**:

1. **URL-based Tasks** (`is_params: false`)
    
2. **Parameter-based Tasks** (`is_params: true`)
    

Use either method depending on your data source, regional targeting needs, or scale of operation.

➡️ Task keys and structure can be found in the [Get crawler parameters](#f0170945-174a-433b-b24d-b35731e78f4e) section.  
➡️ Allowed values for location fields like `country`, `region`, and `district` are available in the [Get geolocation](#2446c796-2263-4d38-b71e-ee9eb2729d42) section.

### 📌 **Task Key Summary**

| Key                  | Type   | Required | `is_params` | Description                                         |
| -------------------- | ------ | -------- | ----------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| `url`                | string | No       | false       | Google Maps search or place URL                     |
| `city`               | string | Yes      | true        | City name and postal code (e.g., `"Anaheim 92801"`) |
| `category`           | string | Yes      | true        | Business category (e.g., `"restaurant"`)            |
| `country`            | string | No       | true        | Country (e.g., `"United States"`)                   |
| `region`             | string | No       | true        | State or province                                   |
| `district`           | string | No       | true        | County or local district                            |

### 📚 **Additional References**

* 📄 [Get crawler parameters](#f0170945-174a-433b-b24d-b35731e78f4e): for all supported task keys and their structure.
* 🌍 [Get geolocation](#2446c796-2263-4d38-b71e-ee9eb2729d42): for retrieving valid values for `country`, `region`, `district`, and `city`.
